summary: DHH reports that his arm sling has come off after an injury, allowing him to type with both hands again. While recovering, he managed 25-30 words per minute one-handed but found it exhausting. During his injury, he paired with another programmer named Lau who handled the typing while DHH directed. He's grateful for the arrangement that kept work progressing and thanks both his programming partner and well-wishers.
key_points: One-handed typing proved feasible at 25-30 WPM but physically exhausting
Pair programming served as a practical workaround for a physical limitation, with one person talking and the other typing
The collaborative arrangement was slower but kept progress moving where otherwise none would have happened
money_quotes: He did the typing, I did the talking.
Much slower than working directly with the keyboard, but we made progress were otherwise none would have been had.
key_insight: Pair programming can serve as a practical adaptation to physical constraints, keeping productivity alive when solo work isn't possible.
